behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in local lint rule logic over template text, not module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check" (single local check), and no lower level than Unit exists.
behaviors.test_description: Test the lint function with in-memory template fixtures containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md; assert reject/accept outcomes and exact violation locations/messages.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module boundaries (template interpolation + fixture merge + assembler). REAL-INFRA: no real HTTP/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing one formatter could miss leakage introduced by composition. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit due "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures)"; rejected System because this is prompt assembly content validation, not OS/network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real harness prompt-construction path with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert assembled prompt does not include any phase-instruction snippets fingerprinted from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is wiring/state-handoff across loader, prompt builder, and invocation config. REAL-INFRA: local files are sufficient; no real HTTP/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: behavior here is provenance of context source, not model quality. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because behavior says "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" (cross-module handoff). Rejected System because it does not require real external infra beyond normal local file reads.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation path using real skill/plugin file loading and assert effective prompt/context references loaded file content; verify no hard-coded phase rules are injected when file content changes.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in harness orchestration (variant selection + prompt invariance + invocation setup). REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: this behavior checks harness capability, not output quality. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit due "harness can run ... against ... degraded or missing SKILL.md variant" (multi-module orchestration). Rejected System because requirement is local variant swapping and run setup, not OS/network/subprocess semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness with baseline and degraded/missing SKILL.md fixtures while keeping identical user prompt text; assert run starts successfully in each case and recorded prompt text is unchanged across variants.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on model behavior changes induced by skill-context degradation; deterministic stubs would hide this regression. MULTI-STEP: single prompt/response decision, not multi-agent pipeline. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior is about model decision quality under changed skill context ("behavioral test fails"), not OS/network mechanics. Rejected Workflow because description is one user-style prompt run, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model with unchanged user-style prompt against baseline vs degraded SKILL.md context; assert baseline satisfies structural criteria while degraded variant fails the same criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is ordering across model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser modules. REAL-INFRA: local artifact writing is enough; no real HTTP/subprocess needed to prove ordering. LLM-DEP: content quality not under test. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because this is pipeline ordering across multiple components ("before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs"). Rejected System because requirement does not depend on external OS/network behavior beyond normal local I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke harness with a deterministic fake model response; assert raw output artifact is persisted first and parser/extractor consumes from subsequent step, with timestamps/order markers proving pre-parse capture.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is assertion-rule logic correctness on structured payloads. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: none required to verify structural-vs-keyword assertion semantics. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text centers on assertion logic itself ("Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints"), which is testable as a local function; no lower level than Unit exists.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with crafted outputs: cases that contain required keywords but violate schema/role placement must fail, while structurally valid outputs pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: requires real model outputs where structure can drift while keywords remain present; stubs hide this. REAL-INFRA is secondary; critical boundary is model behavior. MULTI-STEP: single live execution path, not sequential agentic pipeline. BIDIRECTIONAL-REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior explicitly hinges on "live model execution" output characteristics, not OS/network mechanics alone. Rejected Workflow because it does not specify multiple agentic steps, only one live execution with stronger assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live model on fixed user-style prompts and compare keyword-only vs structural assertions; verify at least one real regression case passes keyword checks but fails structural checks.
